attributes.description: Alerts when GPU temperature exceeds 83°C. Most data-center GPUs throttle between 83°C and 90°C; sustained high temperature damages the hardware.
attributes.name: [NVIDIA GPU OTel] GPU temperature critical

investigation_guide.blob:
## NVIDIA GPU temperature critical

### What fired
`DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP > 83°C` during the window.

### Why it matters
GPUs throttle to protect themselves above their configured thermal limit. Sustained operation near the limit reduces throughput, increases failure rates, and shortens hardware lifespan.

### Triage
1. Identify the affected host / GPU.
2. Check chassis cooling, fan health, and ambient temperature.
3. Correlate with clock-frequency throttling on the same GPU.
4. Look at neighbour GPUs in the same chassis.

### Remediation
- Improve airflow / replace failed fans.
- Reduce concurrent GPU jobs on the host.
- Migrate the workload to a cooler chassis.

### Tuning
- 83°C threshold; tune to your GPU model's documented throttle threshold.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-nvidia_gpu.otel-default
| WHERE DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP IS NOT NULL
// Threshold in Celsius — adjust based on your GPU model and data center cooling
// Most data center GPUs throttle at 83-90°C
| WHERE DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP > 83
| STATS
    max_temp = MAX(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP),
    avg_temp = AVG(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP)
  BY Hostname, UUID, modelName
| SORT max_temp DESC
